Seattle will be entirely eliminated
by the use Of the Great Northern
tracks. Although the two roads
are under different management,
the same money and general inter-
ests control them, which made the
negotiations easy.


The reported plans come from an
authoritative source.


One of the advantages In favor
of the company is the fact that 1t
will not be necessary to purchase
single home, and only very lit
tie property. All the ground that
must be bought Is owned by John
A. Finch, one of the men who was
in favor of the old N. p. plans for
a dirt fill.


1t Is believed that when the
road gets it's trains through the
city via the Great Northern, Of
ficials will then wait until the
courts force them to separate the
N. P. grade. This, it Is estimated.
will take at least five years. After
the courts have ordered the work,
the question will then be as to the
advisable plan, which 1t is contend
ed will be found to be practically
what the company tried to have
the council approve.
